fleet_narrative: Yesterday (04-20) delivered exceptional pricing with all 32 HEN nodes hitting RT >$100/MWh—a rare all-spike event—driving fleet average to $61.4/MWh amid moderate load (50.4 GW) and declining renewables (14.8 GW wind, 18.2 GW solar). This represents a sharp pivot from recent oversupply days and signals strong discharge economics across the entire portfolio, particularly in West Texas where DART compression reached −36.77.
trend_analysis: Over five days, ERCOT transitioned from negative DART (oversupply on 04-16) through volatile mixed conditions (04-19) to yesterday's sustained spike regime with zero negative-price nodes. West Texas has compressed hardest (−36.77 DART avg yesterday vs. +10.18 on 04-16), indicating congestion and renewable curtailment; North Texas remains less volatile but consistently negative in DART. Load has stabilized near 50 GW while wind dropped 45% from peak, reducing the renewable cushion.

anomalies.detail: DART of −49.43 is outlier-level, suggesting severe localized congestion or transmission constraint pricing in West Texas that warrants real-time dispatch review.
Only top-5 DART node with −9.39 (least negative) despite spiking to >$100/MWh RT, indicating DA underpriced this location relative to intraday tightening.
Despite −11.33 DART, intraday spread of 5.76 suggests real-time price swings within the settlement hour; monitor for operational hedging opportunities.

nodes_to_watch.reason: Extreme −49.43 DART and 10.55 intraday spread signal a structurally constrained node where discharge timing and volume can move margin significantly; prioritize for today's dispatch.
−37.3 DART with low intraday volatility (4.5) suggests stable, predictable high RT pricing; monitor load forecast for sustained premium conditions through today.
Top-5 DART performer (−11.52) with tight 5.65 intraday spread indicates DA-RT price discovery is working; track if North Texas renewable curtailment eases today.

market_conditions: Extremely tight ERCOT market yesterday with 100% of HEN nodes spiking (>$100/MWh RT), zero negative-price pockets, and West Texas experiencing severe localized congestion, all driven by load near 50 GW and renewable output in structural decline.
charging_rationale: With $61.4/MWh fleet average RT, zero negative-price nodes, and all 32 locations >$100/MWh, today's discharge is more lucrative than charging; aggressive discharge posture is warranted unless DA pricing signals mean reversion by mid-day.
